headline: National Service Programme Achieves 23% Racial Integration Target
context: The National Service Training Programme (PLKN), reactivated in 2024, achieved only 23% of its racial integration benchmarks in its first year, according to internal ministry assessment.

cards.big: PLKN was revived to promote national unity among youth.
cards.sub: First-year internal assessment: 23% of racial integration benchmarks met. The programme cost RM600M.

cards.big: Participants self-segregate by ethnicity within 48 hours.
cards.sub: Camp design places participants in mixed dormitories but activities allow voluntary re-segregation.

cards.big: Post-programme surveys show no measurable change in interethnic attitudes.
cards.sub: A 2025 UKM study compared participants and non-participants. No statistically significant difference.

cards.big: RM600M annually could fund 12,000 university diversity scholarships.
cards.sub: Alternative interventions with proven integration outcomes cost less per participant.

cards.big: RM600M for a programme with zero measurable impact on racial integration. The money is spent. The unity is not built.
National unity programmes must be evidence-based. If PLKN cannot demonstrate integration outcomes after a fair trial period, the funds should be redirected to interventions that work.
